title: Unify duplicate SequenceMessage types — merge sequence-diagram.ts and LatencyOverlay types
description: TYPE AUDIT T1+T4: Two separate types exist for the same concept: SequenceMessage (sequence-diagram.ts:11) has {id, from, to, label, type, order} and SeqMessage (SequenceDiagramLatencyOverlay.tsx:15) has {id, from, to, label, latencyMs, isReturn?}. Similarly, SequenceParticipant (name field) vs Participant (label field). These should be ONE base type extended. Create: BaseSequenceMessage with shared fields, extend for each use case. Also unify participant naming.
acceptanceCriteria: Single BaseSequenceMessage type with {id, from, to, label}
SequenceMessage extends Base with {type, order}
LatencyMessage extends Base with {latencyMs, isReturn?}
Single SequenceParticipant type with {id, name, type?} — 'label' renamed to 'name'
Both components import from shared type file
Zero duplicate type definitions for same concept

title: Make UMLAttribute.id and UMLMethod.id required (not optional)
description: TYPE AUDIT T2: UMLAttribute.id and UMLMethod.id are typed as optional (string?) at types.ts:6 and types.ts:13. But class-diagram-model.ts:92 filters by a.id (undefined comparison), and code-to-diagram.ts never generates IDs for parsed attributes. This causes: attributes without IDs can't be individually selected or deleted. Make id required and always generate on creation. class-diagram-model.ts:79 already generates IDs — ensure all code paths do too.
acceptanceCriteria: UMLAttribute.id changed from 'id?: string' to 'id: string'
UMLMethod.id changed from 'id?: string' to 'id: string'
code-to-diagram.ts generates unique IDs for every parsed attribute and method
Pattern data in patterns.ts has IDs for all attributes/methods (or generates on load)
TypeScript compiler shows zero errors after change

title: Extract UMLVisibility type alias and separate UMLClass position from data
description: TYPE AUDIT T2: (1) Visibility '+'|'-'|'#'|'~' is repeated inline in UMLAttribute and UMLMethod — extract to shared type alias. (2) UMLClass mixes identity data (id, name, stereotype) with spatial layout (x, y). This violates SoC — when you clone a pattern, you must manually handle x/y separately. Consider: extract position to a ClassLayout map, or at minimum extract the visibility type.
acceptanceCriteria: type UMLVisibility = '+' | '-' | '#' | '~' exported from types.ts
UMLAttribute.visibility and UMLMethod.visibility use UMLVisibility type
VISIBILITY_ICON, VISIBILITY_KEYWORD constants reference UMLVisibility keys
Optional: UMLClass position extracted to separate concern (ClassPositionMap)

title: Fix autoLayout mutation — should return new array, not mutate in place
description: TYPE AUDIT DATA FLOW: autoLayout() at code-to-diagram.ts:24-33 MUTATES input UMLClass[] in place (classes[i].x = ..., classes[i].y = ...). This violates the immutability contract that class-diagram-model.ts carefully maintains (every function returns new objects). If the same array is referenced elsewhere, this mutation corrupts it. Fix: return a new array with updated positions.
acceptanceCriteria: autoLayout returns a new UMLClass[] with updated x/y (does NOT mutate input)
parseTypeScript and parsePython call autoLayout and use the returned value
Original parsed classes array is not modified
Unit tests verify input array is unchanged after autoLayout call

title: Unify ID generation — single strategy for all LLD identifiers
description: TYPE AUDIT T4 CONSISTENCY: Three different ID generation strategies coexist: (1) uid('cls') with Date.now()+counter in class-diagram-model.ts:23, (2) nextId('ts') with simple counter in code-to-diagram.ts:14, (3) crypto.randomUUID().slice(0,8) in LLDModule.tsx:3203. These have different collision properties and formats. Unify to one strategy: crypto.randomUUID() for runtime IDs (already in the module), deterministic content-based IDs for static data (patterns, problems).
acceptanceCriteria: Single generateId() function exported from a shared utility
All runtime ID generation (class creation, attribute addition) uses it
Static data (patterns, problems) uses deterministic IDs ('singleton-cls-0')
Module-level mutable counters (_counter, _relId, etc.) removed
No ID collisions in any scenario

title: Make DesignPattern.code extensible for additional languages
description: TYPE AUDIT T2 EXTENSIBILITY: DesignPattern.code is typed as { typescript: string; python: string } — hardcoded to exactly 2 languages. Adding Java, Go, or Mermaid export (LLD-077) requires changing the type. Change to: code: Record<string, string> with a CodeLanguage type alias, OR code: { language: string; source: string }[]. This makes adding new languages a data change, not a type change.
acceptanceCriteria: DesignPattern.code typed as Record<CodeLanguage, string> where CodeLanguage is extensible
Existing 'typescript' and 'python' entries preserved
Adding 'mermaid' or 'java' doesn't require type changes
GeneratedCodePanel language toggle adapts to available languages dynamically
TypeScript compiler shows zero errors

title: Move SidebarMode, LLDBottomTab, and component prop types to shared types file
description: TYPE AUDIT T1 ORGANIZATION: SidebarMode (line 825) and LLDBottomTab (line 3145) are defined inline in the 3463-line LLDModule.tsx. 27 *Props interfaces are also inline. When LLD-037 splits the monolith, these types need a shared home. Pre-emptively extract all UI-level type definitions to src/lib/lld/ui-types.ts or src/lib/lld/types.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: SidebarMode and LLDBottomTab exported from types.ts or ui-types.ts
All *Props interfaces remain colocated with their components (standard React pattern)
LLDModule.tsx imports types from shared file
No circular dependencies created
Prepares clean split for LLD-037 monolith refactor
